label: threat
description: Periodic HTTPS beaconing detected from workstation to known Cobalt Strike C2 infrastructure. Connections occur every 60 seconds with jitter, transferring small encoded payloads.

label: threat
description: DNS-based C2 channel detected. Host is issuing high-frequency TXT record queries to a suspicious domain with encoded subdomains consistent with data tunneling.

label: threat
description: Sliver C2 implant communication detected over HTTP. Workstation making POST requests with base64-encoded body to a recently registered domain hosted on bulletproof infrastructure.

label: benign
description: Periodic HTTPS connections detected from multiple workstations to external endpoint at regular intervals. Traffic analysis shows consistent 60-second intervals with small payloads — consistent with beaconing pattern.

indicators.explanation: Google Chrome auto-update service performing scheduled update checks

label: benign
description: Workstation generating periodic HTTPS connections to Microsoft infrastructure every 30 seconds. Pattern resembles C2 beaconing with consistent intervals and small encoded payloads.

indicators.explanation: Windows Update service telemetry and update check traffic

label: threat
description: High-velocity credential stuffing attack against VPN portal. Over 2,400 failed login attempts in 10 minutes from a single external IP using a rotating list of usernames, followed by 3 successful authentications.

label: threat
description: Distributed password spraying attack against Exchange OWA. Multiple source IPs attempting a small set of common passwords across hundreds of accounts over 4 hours.

label: threat
description: RDP brute force from internal compromised host targeting domain controllers. Single workstation generating thousands of authentication failures against NTLM over SMB.

label: benign
description: Multiple rapid authentication failures detected from single internal IP against SSO portal. 47 failed logins in 3 minutes for the same user account followed by a successful login.

indicators.explanation: User's password manager (1Password) auto-filling expired cached credentials before manual password update

label: benign
description: Automated authentication failure spike detected against service accounts. 120 failed Kerberos authentications per minute for 3 service accounts from application servers.

indicators.explanation: Service account password rotation by IT — Group Policy pushed new passwords but app configs not yet updated

label: threat
description: Targeted spear phishing email with malicious macro-enabled attachment delivered to 5 Finance department users. Sender domain uses homoglyph substitution to impersonate CFO.

indicators.subject: Q4 Budget Revision - Urgent Action Required

label: threat
description: Credential harvesting campaign targeting IT department. Emails contain links to fake Microsoft 365 login page hosted on compromised WordPress site. Two users have submitted credentials.

indicators.subject: Action Required: Verify Your Microsoft 365 Account

label: threat
description: QR code phishing (quishing) campaign delivered via email. Email impersonates HR department requesting employees scan QR code for benefits enrollment. QR code resolves to credential harvesting page.

indicators.subject: Open Enrollment 2026 - Scan QR Code to Enroll

label: benign
description: Bulk email flagged as potential phishing. External sender with marketing domain sending identical content to 200+ employees. Contains tracking pixels and external links.

indicators.subject: Your Exclusive Invite: 2026 Industry Summit
indicators.explanation: Legitimate marketing email from company's HubSpot-integrated marketing platform for annual industry conference

label: benign
description: Email with external links flagged by DLP. HR vendor sending benefits enrollment links to all employees. SPF/DKIM/DMARC all pass, domain registered 4 years ago.

indicators.subject: Action Required: Complete Your 2026 Benefits Enrollment
indicators.explanation: Authorized HR benefits vendor (BenefitFocus) sending legitimate annual enrollment notifications

label: threat
description: Pass-the-hash attack detected. Compromised workstation using harvested NTLM hashes to authenticate to multiple servers via SMB. Accessing ADMIN$ shares on 12 hosts within 5 minutes.

label: threat
description: WMI-based lateral movement detected. Attacker using compromised domain admin credentials to execute remote commands on servers via WMI. Deploying secondary payload to multiple hosts.

label: threat
description: PSExec lateral movement detected. Remote service creation and execution observed from a single source host to multiple targets. PSEXESVC service installed on target machines.

label: benign
description: SSH connections from single internal host to 15 servers in rapid succession. Authentication uses key-based auth. Source IP belongs to CI/CD build server making deployment connections.

indicators.explanation: Jenkins CI/CD pipeline executing automated deployment to staging environment via Ansible playbook

label: benign
description: WinRM connections detected from IT admin workstation to 8 servers. PowerShell remoting sessions established using domain admin credentials during maintenance window.

indicators.explanation: Authorized IT administrator performing scheduled Windows Server patching during approved maintenance window

label: threat
description: Ransomware execution detected. Process encrypting files across network shares with .locked extension. Ransom note dropped in every directory. Volume shadow copies deleted.

label: threat
description: Emotet dropper detected. Macro-enabled document spawned PowerShell which downloaded and executed secondary payload. Process hollowing observed in svchost.exe.

label: threat
description: Keylogger malware detected. Unknown process capturing keystrokes and writing to hidden log file. Process injected into explorer.exe, sending captured data to external server every 5 minutes.

label: benign
description: Endpoint protection flagged suspicious DLL injection in browser process. chrome.exe loading non-standard DLL from AppData. DLL modifies network traffic patterns.

indicators.explanation: Grammarly browser extension native messaging host DLL — approved enterprise software

label: benign
description: Heuristic detection triggered by executable modifying system files and registry keys. Process performing bulk file operations with elevated privileges across system directories.

indicators.explanation: Windows Update Trusted Installer applying cumulative security update KB5034441

label: threat
description: Large-scale data exfiltration via HTTPS. Workstation uploaded 4.7 GB to anonymous file sharing service over 2 hours. User accessed sensitive file shares immediately prior to upload.

label: threat
description: DNS exfiltration tunnel detected. Anomalous volume of DNS queries with encoded data in subdomain labels. Total estimated data exfiltrated: 850 MB over 6 hours via DNS TXT records.

label: benign
description: Abnormal outbound data transfer detected. Server uploading 12 GB to external cloud storage endpoint over 3 hours. Sustained high-bandwidth HTTPS connection to AWS S3.

indicators.explanation: Veeam backup agent performing scheduled nightly offsite backup to company's AWS S3 bucket

label: benign
description: DLP alert triggered by large file upload. User sending 2.1 GB archive to external cloud service. Sustained upload to OneDrive for Business.

indicators.explanation: Engineer syncing large project repository to corporate OneDrive for Business — approved by data classification policy

label: benign
description: Outbound data spike detected from development server. 8 GB transferred to external registry over 90 minutes. Traffic is container image pushes to company's container registry.

indicators.explanation: CI/CD pipeline pushing newly built Docker images to Google Artifact Registry as part of release process

label: threat
description: Kerberoasting attack detected. Service ticket requests for multiple SPNs from a single workstation using RC4 encryption. Offline cracking of service account passwords suspected.

label: threat
description: Token impersonation and privilege escalation via PrintSpoofer exploit. Low-privilege service account escalated to SYSTEM on web server. Named pipe impersonation detected.

label: benign
description: Privilege elevation detected. Service account granted SeDebugPrivilege and added to local Administrators group on 5 servers simultaneously.

indicators.explanation: IT admin adding monitoring service account to Administrators group per approved change ticket for Datadog agent deployment

label: benign
description: UAC bypass attempt detected. Process requesting elevated privileges through auto-elevation of trusted Microsoft binaries. Multiple elevation prompts from single workstation.

indicators.explanation: IT administrator using Microsoft Management Console snap-ins for routine server management tasks

label: benign
description: Suspicious token manipulation detected. Process creating tokens with elevated privileges using LogonUser API. Multiple SYSTEM-level tokens generated from service process.

indicators.explanation: SQL Server service impersonating connected users for row-level security enforcement — normal RDBMS behavior

label: threat
description: Active Directory enumeration detected. BloodHound/SharpHound data collection observed from compromised workstation. LDAP queries enumerating all users, groups, sessions, and ACLs.

label: threat
description: Network reconnaissance and port scanning from internal host. Sequential SYN scans across entire /24 subnet targeting common service ports. Consistent with Nmap SYN scan pattern.

label: benign
description: Port scanning activity detected from security team subnet. Comprehensive TCP/UDP scan of production network targeting all 65535 ports on multiple hosts.

indicators.explanation: Tenable Nessus vulnerability scanner performing scheduled PCI-DSS quarterly compliance scan

label: benign
description: LDAP enumeration spike detected. Single host performing thousands of LDAP queries against domain controller, enumerating user objects and group memberships.

indicators.explanation: CrowdStrike Falcon sensor performing scheduled Active Directory identity threat detection baseline scan

label: benign
description: Network discovery activity from IT admin workstation. ARP scans and ICMP sweeps across multiple subnets during business hours. SNMP queries to network devices.

indicators.explanation: SolarWinds network discovery module performing scheduled asset inventory refresh for CMDB

label: threat
description: Malicious scheduled task created for persistence. Task set to run at system startup executing encoded PowerShell from hidden directory. Task name mimics legitimate Windows task.

label: threat
description: WMI event subscription persistence mechanism detected. Permanent WMI event consumer created that executes malicious script when any user logs on. Classic fileless persistence technique.

label: benign
description: New scheduled task created with SYSTEM privileges. Task configured to run daily at 2:00 AM executing script from Program Files. Created by IT admin account.

indicators.explanation: IT admin creating scheduled disk cleanup task per approved change request to address low disk space alerts

label: benign
description: Registry Run key modification detected. New entry added to HKLM\...\Run by software installer. Executable set to launch at user logon.

indicators.explanation: CrowdStrike Falcon EDR agent MSI installer adding startup persistence — approved endpoint security software

label: benign
description: New Windows service installed with automatic start. Service binary located in standard Program Files path. Installed by SCCM deployment.

indicators.explanation: SCCM deploying Splunk Universal Forwarder as part of approved enterprise log collection rollout

label: threat
description: Windows Event Log tampering detected. Security event log cleared and audit policies disabled on domain controller. Attacker covering tracks after credential theft.

label: threat
description: AMSI bypass and EDR unhooking detected. Process patching amsi.dll and ntdll.dll in memory to disable security monitoring. Subsequent execution of Mimikatz in unmonitored state.

label: benign
description: Process modifying security tool configuration. Exclusions added to Windows Defender real-time protection for multiple directories and file extensions.

indicators.explanation: IT admin adding Defender exclusions for CI/CD build directories to reduce CPU overhead during compilation — approved change

label: benign
description: Firewall rule modification detected. Multiple inbound allow rules created on web server. Rules permit traffic on non-standard ports from external IPs.

indicators.explanation: IT admin opening ports for new API service deployment per approved change request and architecture review

label: benign
description: Sysmon configuration modified. New exclusion rules added filtering out specific process names and paths from event logging. Changes applied across multiple servers via GPO.

indicators.explanation: Security team tuning Sysmon configuration to reduce false positive event volume per SOC request — approved by CISO
